answer:
**Viṣṇu Smṛti Chapter 14** prescribes the procedure for the ordeal of consecrated water (*kośa*), one of the judicial ordeals (*vyavahārapramāṇa*) used to determine guilt or innocence.[^1]

The specific steps are as follows:

- The accused worships fierce deities and drinks three handfuls of water from their lustral bath (*kośodaka*), while facing the installed deity and declaring, "This was not done by me."[^2]
- If, within two or three weeks, the accused suffers a disease, fire, death of a kinsman, or terror from the king, they are deemed guilty (impure). Otherwise, they are innocent (pure).[^3]
- A righteous king should honor those proven pure by the ordeal.[^4]

[^1]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 14, [translation](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/14/translation/).

[^2]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 14, [paragraphs p-005f08 to p-c5a5e4](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/14/translation/#p-005f08).

[^3]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 14, [paragraphs p-b064d6 to p-b86f28](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/14/translation/#p-b064d6).

[^4]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chapter 14, [p-b86f28](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/14/translation/#p-b86f28).
